title: Civil Service Annual Leave Policy
text: Civil servants are entitled to a minimum of 25 days of paid annual leave per year. This entitlement increases by one day for each year of service, up to a maximum of 30 days. Annual leave is in addition to public and privilege holidays. New entrants typically join on the minimum entitlement. Part-time staff receive a pro-rata allowance based on their contracted hours. Leave must usually be agreed with a line manager in advance and is subject to the needs of the business.

title: Civil Service Pension Scheme Overview
text: The Civil Service offers a Defined Benefit (alpha) pension scheme. Employer contributions are set at 28.97% of pensionable earnings. The scheme provides an index-linked pension on retirement, ill-health benefits, and benefits for dependants on death. Member contributions are tiered according to salary. The pension builds up each year based on a proportion of pensionable earnings, rather than depending on stock-market performance, which distinguishes a defined benefit scheme from a defined contribution one.

title: Security Check (SC) Clearance Guidance
text: Security Check (SC) clearance normally requires five years of UK residency within the preceding five years. This is not an absolute requirement, but where an individual has not lived in the UK for that period, supplementary checks may be required, which can extend the timeline or, in some cases, make clearance impossible. SC clearance involves a check of criminal records, credit history, and security service records. It is required for posts with regular access to SECRET assets. Baseline Personnel Security Standard (BPSS) checks are a prerequisite.

title: Cabinet Office Workplace Attendance Policy
text: Cabinet Office policy is that a minimum of 60% of working time should be spent at an employee's principal workplace. For some roles, due to their nature and business need, this expectation may rise to as much as 100%. Requirements to attend other locations for official business count towards this attendance level. Flexible working options are available and can be discussed with the vacancy holder, though preferred patterns are not guaranteed and depend on business need.

title: Guidance on AI Use in Job Applications
text: Artificial intelligence can be a useful tool to support a job application. However, all examples and statements provided must be truthful, factually accurate, and taken directly from the applicant's own experience. Where plagiarism is identified, including presenting the ideas or experiences of others or material generated by artificial intelligence as one's own, applications may be withdrawn. Internal candidates may additionally be subject to disciplinary action.
